task_completion.description: InVesalius 3 is open with the CT Cranium DICOM scan loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 application window is visible, displaying 2D medical imaging slices (axial, coronal, sagittal) of a skull.

task_completion.description: A 3D bone surface model of the skull is generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer pane (usually the bottom right quadrant) displays a 3D rendered model of a human skull.

task_completion.description: The 3D linear measurement tool is activated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A ruler or measurement tool icon is highlighted or selected in the InVesalius toolbar or menu.

task_completion.description: Measurement points are placed on the widest points of the left and right cheekbones (Zygomatic Arches) on the 3D model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A measurement line is drawn across the face of the 3D skull model, specifically connecting the outermost lateral curves of the left and right cheekbones.

task_completion.description: The measurement value for the bizygomatic breadth is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A numeric value (typically between 110.0 and 160.0 mm) is visible next to the measurement line on the 3D model or in a measurement list panel.

task_completion.description: The project is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Project As' dialog shows the path '/home/ga/Documents' and filename 'skull_3d_measure.inv3', or the main InVesalius window title bar updates to show 'skull_3d_measure.inv3'.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius GUI to generate the 3D model and perform the measurement.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with InVesalius menus, toolbars, and the 3D viewer, rather than just terminal commands.

integrity.description: The measurement was performed natively within the InVesalius 3D viewer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement line and text match the native InVesalius rendering style and are attached to the 3D model, not drawn over the screen using an external image editor.

integrity.description: The project was saved using the application's save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog is visible, ensuring an actual .inv3 project file is created rather than a dummy file generated via the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly /home/ga/Documents/skull_3d_measure.inv3. The DICOM series loaded is ct_cranium/0051. A valid bizygomatic width measurement (distance between the widest points of the zygomatic arches) should typically fall between 110 mm and 160 mm.
